mechanism_narrative: A sustained 3-month decline in the federal funds rate signals an easing monetary cycle that compresses discount rates applied to equity cash flows, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A 3-month increase in the unemployment rate above 6.5% signals a weakening labor market that reduces consumer spending and corporate profitability, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns in periods with UNRATE > 6.5% are not significantly lower than baseline (t-test p < 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A 3-month increase in housing starts above 800,000 units signals a strengthening housing market that boosts consumer confidence and corporate investment, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns in periods with HOUST > 800,000 are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p < 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A 3-month decline in industrial production below 105 signals a weakening supply chain that reduces corporate profitability and investment, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns in periods with INDPRO < 105 are not significantly lower than baseline (t-test p < 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A 3-month increase in payroll employment above 140,000,000 signals a strengthening labor market that boosts consumer spending and corporate profitability, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns in periods with PAYEMS > 140,000,000 are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p < 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A 3-month increase in real disposable personal income above 12,000 signals a strengthening consumer market that boosts corporate investment and profitability, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns in periods with RSAFS > 12,000 are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p < 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A 3-month increase in the consumer price index above 220 signals a strengthening inflationary environment that boosts interest rates and reduces corporate profitability, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns in periods with CPIAUCSL > 220 are not significantly lower than baseline (t-test p < 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A 3-month increase in the industrial production index above 110 signals a strengthening supply chain that boosts corporate profitability and investment, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns in periods with INDPRO > 110 are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p < 0.05).
